conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility rules for Child Benefit. Now, let me ask you some questions to determine your eligibility.

**Do you have any children under 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, attending college, sixth form, or an approved training course)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any approved education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on the rules I've reviewed:

- Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16
- It can continue for children aged 16-19 **if** they are in approved education or training
- If a child is 18 years old and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the eligibility criteria

Before I give you my final decision, let me ask one more question to ensure I have the complete picture:

**When did Alex turn 18, and when did they leave education or training?** (This is important because Child Benefit can continue for up to 20 weeks after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training, but I need to confirm the timeline in Alex's case)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 18 a while back and left school some time ago - they're not in that 20-week extension period anymore.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. I now have all the information I need to make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason is:** Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit is available for:
- Children under 16, OR
- Children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training

Since Alex has left education and is beyond the 20-week extension period that can apply after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training, they do not meet the eligibility criteria.

However, if Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future while still under 20 years old, you would be able to claim Child Benefit again at that time.

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change, please don't hesitate to contact the Child Benefit Office.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally qualifies for children under 16, or for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex has left education and is no longer within the 20-week extension period that applies when a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training. Therefore, Alex does not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. Alex is beyond the 20-week extension period following leaving education. As Alex does not meet the criteria of being either under 16 or aged 16-19 in approved education/training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally qualifies for children under 16, or for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex has left education and is no longer within the 20-week extension period that applies when a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training. Therefore, Alex does not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. Alex is beyond the 20-week extension period following leaving education. As Alex does not meet the criteria of being either under 16 or aged 16-19 in approved education/training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
